div.h1: Circular Work Queue: Enqueue & Dequeue Operations
div.p: WORK_QUEUE_CAPACITY = 8 · O(1) enqueue · O(1) dequeue · No element shifting

g.text: Step 2: Enqueue Item A → queue[tail=0] = A, tail = (0+1)%8 = 1, count = 1
Step 3: Enqueue B → queue[1]=B, Enqueue C → queue[2]=C (tail=3, count=3)
Step 4: Dequeue → item = queue[head=0] = A, head = (0+1)%8 = 1, count = 2
Step 5: Enqueue D…H + wrap-around → tail reaches [7], next goes to [0] (count=8, FULL)
Step 6: Enqueue attempt when count==8 → return -1 → caller sends 503

div.h2: Ring Buffer Invariants (must hold under pool->lock)

ul.li: count == (tail - head + CAPACITY) % CAPACITY when count < CAPACITY
count == CAPACITY when tail == head && full
Full: count == WORK_QUEUE_CAPACITY (head == tail after wrap)
Distinguish empty vs full using count, NOT by comparing head and tail alone
tail = (tail + 1) % CAPACITY — compile target: bitmask & (CAPACITY-1) at -O2

div.p: head = 0 · tail = 0 · count = 0 Queue is EMPTY head == tail && count == 0 → empty condition

head = 0 · tail = 1 · count = 1 pool->queue[pool->tail] = item ← write at tail pool->tail = (pool->tail + 1) % WORK_QUEUE_CAPACITY ← advance tail pool->count++ pthread_cond_signal(&pool->not_empty) ← wake one worker
head = 0 · tail = 3 · count = 3 Items A, B, C occupying consecutive slots No element shifting — each enqueue writes exactly 1 slot

item = pool->queue[pool->head] ← value copy (24 bytes) pool->head = (pool->head + 1) % WORK_QUEUE_CAPACITY pool->count-- pthread_cond_signal(&pool->not_full) pthread_mutex_unlock(&pool->lock) ← handle item OUTSIDE the lock

Wrap-around: tail was at [7] (item H) Next: tail = (7+1) % 8 = 0 ← item I written to [0] Then: tail = (0+1) % 8 = 1 Now tail == head == 1 with count == 8 → FULL Full condition: pool->count >= WORK_QUEUE_CAPACITY (NOT tail == head alone — that is also the empty condition)
